title: Train a same-lap groupwise ranker instead of only pointwise classifiers
summary: At each `Year,Race,LapNumber`, only a few cars are truly in the pit window.
rationale: At each `Year,Race,LapNumber`, only a few cars are truly in the pit window. Learning-to-rank objectives can directly optimize within-group ordering, which is closer to the decision problem than treating every row independently.
implementation_hint: Use `qid = Year__Race__LapNumber` and train XGBoost LTR or CatBoost ranking objectives so positives outrank negatives inside each lap-group. Blend its scores with the best pointwise model; rank-based blending often helps AUC even if raw probabilities are miscalibrated.
expected_effect: Should improve relative ordering among cars facing the same track conditions, especially when same-lap context drives the decision.
risk: Many groups may have zero positives, making ranking data sparse; scores may need calibration before averaging with probabilistic models.
